---
title: "Home"
about:
  template: jolla
  image: images/profile.png
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/serena-128
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/serena-e-606aa122a/
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:serena.emedeke2@mail.dcu.ie
---

## Welcome to My Portfolio

Hi, I’m **Serena Emedeke** — a student developing practical skills in **Python, data analysis, Quarto, Git, and digital presentation**.

::: {.callout-note}
This portfolio demonstrates my ability to **analyse data, build structured digital projects, solve technical problems, and present work in a clear and professional format**.
:::

## What I Bring

- Strong foundation in **Python and data analysis**
- Ability to communicate technical results clearly
- Experience using **Git and version control**
- A structured and professional approach to project work

## Highlights

::: {.section-card}
### 📊 Data Analysis Projects
Practical Python projects demonstrating data organisation, interpretation, and visualisation.
:::

::: {.section-card}
### 🧠 Problem Solving and Reflection
Evidence of critical thinking, troubleshooting, and continuous improvement through reflective practice.
:::

::: {.section-card}
### 📄 Professional Portfolio
A polished overview of my background, skills, CV, and contact information.
:::

## Featured Projects

Each project demonstrates a structured approach to data analysis, including objectives, implementation, interpretation, and reflection.

::: {.section-card}
### 📊 Student Performance Analysis
A Python project analysing the relationship between study time and academic performance, using data visualisation to identify trends and support conclusions.

**Tools:** Python, Pandas, Matplotlib

[View Project](projects/list/project-1.html)
:::

::: {.section-card}
### 💰 Monthly Budget Analysis
A Python project analysing income and expenses to identify spending patterns and support financial decision-making through data visualisation.

**Tools:** Python, Pandas, Matplotlib

[View Project](projects/list/project-2.html)
:::
